name: Do-Ahead Party Mashed Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Dairy sour cream OR Plain yogurt
2 tsp Onion salt OR 1 ts onion salt, 1 ts garlic salt
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and quarter the potatoes. You will have approximately 7-8 cups of peeled potatoes.
2. Place the potatoes in a large saucepan with enough water to cover them. Bring to a boil and boil until tender, about 20-25 minutes.
3. Drain well.
4. Mash the potatoes until smooth using a potato masher or electric mixer.
5. Mix cream cheese with sour cream or yogurt, onion salt (or garlic salt), and pepper in a separate bowl.
6. Beat the mixture into the mashed potatoes and beat again with a whisk until smooth and fluffy. Taste and adjust seasoning by adding a pinch of plain salt if needed.
7. Transfer to a two-quart casserole dish or refrigerator container. Cool and cover.
8. To serve, place the desired amount in a greased casserole dish, dot with butter or margarine, and sprinkle with paprika or top with lots of cheese (freshly grated, if possible).
9. Bake at 350°F for 30-45 minutes, uncovered, until heated through. Makes 8-10 servings.
Tastes best if refrigerated 12-24 hours before baking. Can be kept in a covered refrigerator container for up to two weeks.
10. POTATOES WITH CHIVES: Instead of onion salt, use garlic salt or onion salt, a pinch of plain salt, and one tablespoon minced freeze-dried or fresh chives.
